skill.name: r-development
skill.description: Guides modern R development with tidyverse patterns, rlang metaprogramming, performance profiling, package development, and R object systems.
skill.summary: Modern R development guidance for tidyverse code, packages, performance, and object systems.
skill.author: CodingKaiser

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are false positives caused by markdown backticks, R syntax examples, and ordinary documentation terms. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, command execution workflow, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown and R Syntax False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to markdown inline code, R code fences, native pipe examples, and tidyverse syntax. These examples do not instruct the agent to run shell commands or execute untrusted system input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are visibly markdown or R examples. No shell command invocation pattern or user-controlled command execution path was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Word-Match False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak algorithm alerts appear to match substrings inside words such as description, design, or DESCRIPTION file guidance. The reviewed locations do not recommend DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is normal R package documentation terminology. There is no cryptographic code, hashing guidance, or security-sensitive algorithm choice in context.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Findings Are rlang Pronoun Documentation
low_findings.description: The sensitive access alerts are caused by rlang `.env` pronoun examples. In context, `.env` distinguishes data variables from environment variables inside R expressions and is not file or secret access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explain tidy evaluation scoping and use local numeric variables. No `.Renviron`, `Sys.getenv`, credential name, or network sink appears in context.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance and Obfuscation Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance and obfuscation alerts point to R performance terms, object system documentation, and repeated bracket operators in rlang examples. They do not collect host information or hide executable payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static signals are explained by normal R documentation and operator-heavy examples. No encoded payload, host enumeration routine, or prompt injection text was found.

content.value_statement: Modern R code changes quickly, and older patterns can make projects harder to maintain. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward current tidyverse, rlang, performance, and package practices.
actual_capabilities: Recommends modern tidyverse patterns, including native pipe, `join_by()`, `.by`, `pick()`, and `reframe()`.
Explains rlang data masking, embracing, injection, dynamic dots, and `.data` or `.env` pronouns.
Guides profiling and benchmarking with tools such as profvis, bench, dtplyr, purrr, and vctrs.
Provides R package development guidance for APIs, dependencies, tests, documentation, and DESCRIPTION files.
Compares R object systems, including S3, S4, S7, R6, and vctrs for class design decisions.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not an automated R runtime or package builder.
It assumes modern R and tidyverse versions for many recommendations.
It does not replace project-specific profiling, testing, or CRAN policy review.
It does not include domain-specific statistical modeling guidance.

use_cases.title: Modernize Legacy R Scripts
use_cases.description: Convert older base R or magrittr-heavy workflows into readable tidyverse code using native pipe and current dplyr patterns.

use_cases.title: Design Package APIs
use_cases.description: Plan exported functions, dependency choices, validation, tests, documentation, and namespace strategy for an R package.

use_cases.title: Improve Slow R Workflows
use_cases.description: Choose profiling tools, benchmark alternatives, and apply vectorization, dtplyr, or parallel mapping when they fit the workload.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh an R Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this R data pipeline and suggest modern tidyverse changes. Prefer native pipe, `.by`, and current dplyr syntax where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Tidyverse Helper
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an R helper function for this workflow. Use tidy evaluation safely and explain when to use `{{}}`, `.data`, or `!!`.

prompt_templates.title: Profile and Optimize R Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this slow R workflow. Recommend a profiling plan, benchmark candidates, and identify changes that preserve readability.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an R Package API
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an R package API for this feature set. Include dependency strategy, validation, tests, documentation, and object system choices.

output_examples.input: Modernize a summarization script that uses magrittr pipes and repeated grouping.
output_examples.output: A migration plan that replaces legacy pipes with native pipe, uses `.by` for local grouping, and calls out tests for changed joins.

output_examples.input: Choose between S3, S7, R6, and vctrs for a new package class.
output_examples.output: A decision summary that maps the object needs to each R system and recommends the simplest maintainable design.

output_examples.input: Speed up an R workflow that processes many large data frames.
output_examples.output: A profiling-first optimization plan with benchmark targets, memory checks, and candidate use of dtplyr or parallel mapping.

best_practices: Ask for the current R version and package versions before applying modern syntax broadly.
Profile and benchmark realistic workloads before changing code for performance.
Pair API design changes with tests, documentation, and input validation.
anti_patterns: Do not rewrite readable R code for speed without profiling evidence.
Do not use tidy evaluation operators when simple `.data[[name]]` access is clearer.
Do not add broad tidyverse dependencies when a small package dependency is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill run R code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for an AI assistant to write or review R code.

faq.question: Which R style does it prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers modern tidyverse style, native pipe, dplyr 1.1 features, and explicit tidy evaluation patterns.

faq.question: Can it help create R packages?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers API design, dependency choices, tests, documentation, DESCRIPTION files, and namespace guidance.

faq.question: Can it optimize slow R code?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes profiling first, then targeted benchmarking, vectorization, memory improvements, or parallel work.

faq.question: Does it support R object-oriented design?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares S3, S4, S7, R6, and vctrs for common package and class design needs.

faq.question: Is it only for tidyverse users?
faq.answer: No. It also discusses base R trade-offs, package structure, performance tools, and object systems.
